reasoning: The objective_card_v07 panel renders at roughly 260×110 px screen-space while visible player-scale sprites (tile-grid reference) are ~50-60 px wide, making the card ~4-5× player width. For a labeled instruction panel this is borderline acceptable—it must be legible—but the card's footprint covers a meaningful slice of the active playfield and partially overlaps the creature sprite on the left. The decorative pixel-art border suits the game aesthetic, but the panel sits flat in the mid-scene with no depth cue, giving a 'HUD overlay' feel rather than 'toy diorama object.' Readability is good; the main concern is that the panel crowds the scene at its current size and anchor position. Reducing width by ~20% or repositioning to a screen edge would push this to a clean pass.
